```json
[
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Buildout Acceleration Bullish Play",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap API daily charger counts by region through openchargemap adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Public EV charger network expansion correlates with infrastructure investment cycles and consumer EV adoption confidence. Rapid week-over-week growth in charger availability signals macro tailwind for energy transition.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Charger installation is industrial capex; growth signals infrastructure spending momentum benefiting industrial equipment and construction."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly charger count increase exceeds prior 8-week rolling average by 15% or more",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or if weekly growth falls below 5% for 2 consecutive weeks",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Charger deployment fluctuates seasonally and by funding cycles; threshold of 15% growth above 8-week MA fires 2–3 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Natural Gas Price Spike Industrial Input Stress Signal",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series ID DHHNGSP (Henry Hub Natural Gas Spot Price) through fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Natural gas price spikes compress margins for chemical, fertilizer, and power generation firms. Sharp intraweek rallies often reverse within 1–2 weeks as supply/demand rebalances.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Industrial production and utilities are major gas consumers; price shocks raise input costs and trigger margin compression."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If natural gas price rises more than 8% in a single week and closes above 5-week high",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or if price falls below the 5-week moving average",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Natural gas is volatile; 8% weekly moves occur 3–4 times per quarter, especially in winter and summer demand spikes."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Unemployment Spike Google Trends Job Search Urgency",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest for 'unemployment benefits' and 'job openings near me' through google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sudden spikes in unemployment-related and urgent job search queries precede or coincide with labor market deterioration. High search volume indicates worker stress and hiring slowdown.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Job insecurity depresses consumer discretionary spending; labor stress reduces purchasing power."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If combined search index for both terms rises above 70th percentile of 52-week range in a single week",
      "exit": "After 14 calendar days or if index falls back below 50th percentile for 2 consecutive weeks",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Job market anxiety spikes occur monthly around earnings announcements, layoff waves, and macro uncertainty; threshold is loose enough to fire 2–3 times per month."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Freight Bill Of Lading Cost Inflation Pressure",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series ID TOTALSA (Total Business Inventories) and FRED EXCRESW (Advance Retail Sales) ratio divergence through fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When inventory growth outpaces sales growth, logistics firms hold excess cargo and rates compress. Sudden sales acceleration forces rapid restocking, spiking freight demand and rates.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Freight logistics (rail, trucking, shipping) benefit from cargo volume spikes and higher utilization rates."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly inventory-to-sales ratio drops below 2-month low and retail sales surprise positively by 2%+ week-over-week",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or if inventory ratio rises back above 2-month moving average",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Inventory cycles reset quarterly; sales surprises and ratio inversions fire 2–4 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Travel Mobility Pent-Up Demand Google Trends Surge",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest for 'vacation packages', 'flight deals', and 'hotel bookings' through google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sharp spikes in travel booking searches indicate sudden pent-up demand release, often following lockdowns, price drops, or seasonal transitions. Correlates with airline and hospitality stock strength.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Travel and leisure are discretionary; search spikes signal consumer confidence and spending willingness."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If combined travel search index exceeds 65th percentile of 13-week rolling window",
      "exit": "After 12 trading days or if index falls to below 40th percentile for 2 consecutive weeks",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Travel searches spike seasonally (holidays, spring break, summer) and after price drops; threshold fires 2–3 times per month."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Copper Price Momentum Mean Reversion Industrial Cycle",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Yahoo Finance daily prices for CU (Copper Futures ETF) or commodity copper index through price_only adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Copper is a leading cyclical indicator. Sharp 3–5 day rallies above the 50-day moving average often reverse within a week as overextension unwinds, creating mean reversion trades.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLB",
      "rationale": "Copper strength signals manufacturing demand and infrastructure investment; materials and industrials benefit."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If copper price rallies 4%+ in 3 trading days and closes above 50-day MA by 2%+",
      "exit": "After 6 trading days or if price falls back below 50-day MA",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Copper rallies 4%+ in 3 days occur 1–2 times per month on cyclical swings and Fed commentary."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Seismic Activity Uptick Insurance & Realty Volatility Spike",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "USGS Earthquake Hazards Program API daily earthquake counts (magnitude 3.0+) by region through earthquake_activity adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Clusters of seismic events in major metro regions (CA, WA, OK) trigger insurance claim anticipation and property revaluation concerns. Real estate and insurance stocks respond to perceived risk.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLRE",
      "rationale": "Earthquake clusters depress property values and increase insurance costs; REIT valuations compress."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly count of magnitude 3.0+ earthquakes in CA+WA+OK exceeds 52-week 75th percentile (typical: 3+ events)",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or if weekly earthquake count returns to median for 2 consecutive weeks",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seismic clusters occur unpredictably but reliably; threshold fires 1–2 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Medical Care Cost Search Spike Consumer Health Stress",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest for 'medical debt', 'prescription drug cost', 'health insurance denied claim' through google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in healthcare cost and debt-related searches indicate consumer financial stress from medical expenses. Precedes credit card delinquencies and reduced discretionary spending.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Medical cost stress reduces disposable income and consumer confidence, depressing discretionary sector."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If combined healthcare cost search index rises above 60th percentile of 26-week rolling window",
      "exit": "After 14 calendar days or if index falls below 40th percentile for 2 consecutive weeks",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Healthcare cost searches spike during open enrollment, post-deductible resets (Jan), and high-inflation periods; fires 2–3 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Fed Liquidity Drain Rate Shock Dollar Strength Rally",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series ID WIMFSL (Factors Affecting Reserve Balances – Liquidity Drain) through fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sharp increases in Fed reverse repo usage or Treasury bill drains signal sudden liquidity tightening. Dollar strengthens, emerging markets weaken, commodity prices compress.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Dollar strength and liquidity drains compress commodity and emerging market revenues; energy exporters underperform."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If Fed liquidity drain (weekly average) increases 10%+ versus prior week and closes at 10-week high",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or if drain rate falls 5% below the spike peak",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Liquidity drains occur with quarter-end, tax date, and seasonal treasury issuance cycles; 10% spikes fire 2–3 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Credit Card Application Volume Surge Consumer Confidence Inflection",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series ID TERMCBCCALLNS (Credit Card Applications Approved) through fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sudden spikes in credit card applications signal consumer confidence inflection. Rising applications precede consumer spending rallies and credit card company profitability expansion.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Credit card volume and approval rates directly impact bank and fintech lending profitability."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly credit card applications approved exceed 8-week moving average by 12%+ and trend higher for 2 weeks",
      "exit": "After 15 trading days or if approvals fall back below 8-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Card application cycles reset with holiday shopping, back-to-school, and quarterly rate change announcements; fires 2–4 times per quarter."
  }
]
```
